entry.title: Inquiry Regarding Depreciation Area Settings
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support

I am writing to inquire about the depreciation area settings.

For tangible assets, we use the indirect method, while for intangible assets, we apply the direct method. However, as only one primary depreciation area can be designated, it is mandatory to assign the primary depreciation area set for the indirect method in the master data of fixed assets for intangible assets.

Is there a way to exclude this setting, or alternatively, to calculate depreciation for intangible assets using the direct method?

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your guidance.

Best regards

entry.title: EC Diagnostic Tool :SAP Successfactors Onboarding
entry.content:
This Blog was built together with @SanchayitaKundu and @Angela_Romano  .

As you aware Successfactors EC Team has introduced Diagnostic tool analysis for Succesfactors Module in 1H2024 Release. In this blog , we will cover how Diagnostic Blog can be utilized in Onboarding Module.

​This tool enables self-diagnosis and self-analysis of the complete data transaction and workflow processing in a single view, allowing customers or internal users to resolve issues independently and reduce resolution time.

​Supported Transaction Types:

  • Workflows
  • Centralized Services Save Actions
  • Snapshot View
  • Rule Execution View

​RBP to enable tool:

  • Administrator > Diagnostic Tool > Diagnostics Tracing Configuration
  • Administrator > Diagnostic Tool > Workflows Diagnostics Data
  • Administrator > Diagnostic Tool > Centralized Services Diagnostics Data
  • Administrator > Admin Center Permissions > Access Diagnostic Tool 

​

​Note:
We recommend creating a permission group to restrict access to this tool to only a few people/admins. This tool does not respect RBP access at field levels therefore the provided admins can see restricted employee's data. To avoid such issues or concerns please create a separate permission role for this access and give the access to few admins/users so that it can be easily monitored and controlled.

Save Action Transaction:

  • Snapshot View shows the snapshots or the records starting from the fields that were changed till the related changes, rules, end date corrections, forward propagation and final save action. In each stage a tabular data will be shown for all related cross-entities or sub-entities involved. We can compare the field changes in each stage to get an idea of which stage the field values changed.
  • Rule Execution view shows in detail all the records that were filtered out for rule execution with reason, all the rules that were filtered out with reason and all the rules that got executed with timestamp and details of the fields and it's values the rule changed. It gives functionality to search by rule ID or field ID for cases where several rules got executed.
  • After enabling the tool, the issue needs to be replicated and then the transaction trace is available for issue analysis.
  • Tracing will turn off after 15 minutes. The traced data will auto delete after 10 days.
  • The tool doesn't support MDF objects for now.
  • Only onSave rules data are captured.
  • Mass changes, API and imports save transactions are not traced.

Save Action Configuration: Business Action should be Onboarding.

  • In Manage Data, set up the Diagnostics Tracing Configuration MDF object.  You may also use the direct link in diagnostic tool page, where a link to "Enable Tracing" is provided.
  • In Diagnostic Trace Configuration, create new or modify existing trace, by entering any code, status= active and Activate Diagnostic Trace = Yes.
  • Enter user name of user completing the transaction in Requested By field (if using proxy enter that user's name). 
  • Requested For field doesn’t support external onboarding users yet

  • Save the object and then replicate the save action on a user.

  • Once this object is saved from the start time provided the tracing will be active for 15 minutes.

  • Within this time frame, any Save transaction executed by the requested user would be captured and available in the tracing details.

  • Business Action enlists all supported ChangeSources.

    • New Hire Data Review / Access New Hire Data / Personal Data Collection/Final Data Review /Hire Template with standard HRIS Element- Onboarding
    • Manage Pending Hires – Hire/Rehire Processes
    • Offboarding / RHDR Pages /MDF pages is not supported

    • For PDC page :

         A) if manager completed PDC on behalf of onboardee ,then we capture this transaction ? - Yes, since the 'Requested By' is an internal employee and searchable on the tool configuration.   
    •    B)If PDC completed by onboardee ,do we support transaction ? - Currently this information is not captured since 'Requested By' doesn't allow search of External Onboarding Users

Workflow Tracing:

  • The approval or actions on the workflow should be done only from "workflow details page".
  • If the workflows are approved from the "home page quick action" or "mobile device" or "Manage workflow requests" admin tool by taking action on the workflows by admin and changing workflow approvers or status, then such changes will not be traced. In the diagnostic tool for such scenarios only the initiate data with pending workflow status will be shown because other approval or send back actions was not traced.
  • Workflows tracing will start and keep capturing from the date the Role-based permission (RBP) is provided to the Diagnostic tool > Workflow Configuration Data. However, after 10 days of no action on the workflow the tracing data will get auto-deleted. If any action is done on the workflow like approval or send back, the tracing data will stay for + 10 days and so on.
  • Auto-approval or CC role workflows are supported.
  • Workflows based on HRIS Elements are only supported.
  • Workflow trace will be only if the workflow is actually submitted. No simulation tracing is available.

Workflow Tracing Transaction

Search fields for Workflow Tracing:

  • Requested By = user doing the transaction
  • Requested For = user whose data is getting changed in the transaction
  • Date Range = Workflow initiate date Or when the changes on employee was done/ issue replication date. At a time only 3 days can be selected and searched.

Thanks,

Gopinath M

entry.title: Re: Incorrect Stitching
entry.content:
Hi Vysak,
when using batching / stitching, there are special JS commands offered to enable page numbering across the different chunks. Or to cite the documentation:

"""
For all batch output, pages will be numbered consecutively across individual documents, but only when those
documents are rendered using the same template. That is, the first document will start at page one and the second document will continue where the first left off. To enable this, some settings in Designer must be set, as follows.
When designing the template, modify the default scripts for CurrentPage and PageCount as follows:
For CurrentPage, replace
xfa.layout.page(this) with xfa.layout.absPageInBatch(this),

which starts with 0; so if you want to use 1 as the first page, use:
xfa.layout.absPageInBatch(this)+ 1.

For PageCount (total page number), replace:
xfa.layout.pageCount() with xfa.layout.absPageCountInBatch()
"""

Best regards
Pascal

entry.title: Re: Operation UPDATE not supported for LO-H
entry.content:
Hi AustinKloske,

the Handling Unit OData API supports two kinds of Handling Units:
- LO HU (ERP HUs)
- EWM HU

The Handling Unit OData API supports the update of the HU header for EWM HU. But not for LO HU at the moment.

You can check the existing documentation for supported API operations
https://api.sap.com/api/OP_HANDLINGUNIT_0001/documents ->
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e1841426f60f4e50913ec9a64aba8332/9240f938e56542f3a2ceb3fce7c96cda.html

At SAP Customer Influence there is an existing request: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/273456.
If you want, you can vote for it.

As workaround you could use the existing remote Function Module BAPI_HU_CHANGE_HEADER for your purpose.

Best regards,
Barış Yalçın

entry.title: Re: Clarification on Solman Application for SAP Satellite...

Whether it is installed on-premises or in the cloud, the solution manager has the same capabilities. The main difference lies in who hosts the operating system and configuration. For more quesions about the RISE and GROW, please reach out to the sales and ECS team. Best regards, Vera Jiang

entry.title: Request for Guidance on Resolving ChaRM Issue Post-SAP BW Migration to SAP S/4 HANA RISE with SAP.
entry.content:
Hello Solman and SAP Cloud Experts,

I hope this message finds you well.

We have recently completed the migration of our SAP BW system to the SAP S/4 HANA system under the RISE with SAP initiative. As part of this transition, we are currently managing six SID entries for three systems with both the old (pre-migration) and new (post-migration) host names as follows:

  • ABC → before migration, ABC00001 → after migration
  • DEF → before migration, DEF00001 → after migration
  • GHI → before migration, GHI00001 → after migration

While the RFC connections for the pre-migration systems have been manually updated through transaction code SM59, pointing to the new hostnames in the S/4 HANA environment, the system entries in the LMDB still point to the old SIDs (ABC, DEF, GHI).

Moreover, we have encountered an issue when trying to release a Transport Request from the ABC system. The error message "ABC System not found" is being displayed, which indicates that the system is not properly recognized within the current configuration.

I would like to request your assistance in confirming whether I can proceed with the following solutions to resolve or mitigate this issue. Alternatively, if there are additional activities or steps that need to be carried out in such scenarios, I would greatly appreciate your guidance.

Scenario 1: Systems Already Migrated to SAP HANA Private Cloud/SAP2SKY with Existing RFC Connections Updated Manually in SM59 and New System Entries Updated in LMDB

Option A:

  1. Create new RFC connections for the new system entries (<D-SID>00001, <Q-SID>00001, <P-SID>00001) through Managed System Configuration. The following credentials are required from both systems as detailed in Screenshot-1:
    • <D-SID>00001 (Development)
      • SM_<D-SID>00001CLNT<Client no>_READ
      • SM_<D-SID>00001CLNT<Client no>_TMW
      • SM_<D-SID>00001CLNT<Client no>_TRUSTED
      • SM_<D-SID>00001CLNT<Client no>_LOGIN
    • <Q-SID>00001 (Quality)
      • SM_<Q-SID>00001CLNT<Client no>_READ
      • SM_<Q-SID>00001CLNT<Client no>_TMW
      • SM_<Q-SID>00001CLNT<Client no>_TRUSTED
      • SM_<Q-SID>00001CLNT<Client no>_LOGIN
    • <P-SID>00001 (Production)
      • SM_<P-SID>00001CLNT<Client no>_READ
      • SM_<P-SID>00001CLNT<Client no>_TMW
      • SM_<P-SID>00001CLNT<Client no>_TRUSTED
      • SM_<P-SID>00001CLNT<Client no>_LOGIN
  2. Allocate the existing logical component group (<LCG_NAME>) to the new system entries (<D-SID>00001, <Q-SID>00001, and <P-SID>00001).
  3. De-allocate the logical component group (<LCG_NAME>) from the old system entries (<D-SID>, <Q-SID>, and <P-SID>).
  4. Create a new phase cycle with the new systems (<D-SID>00001, <Q-SID>00001, and <P-SID>00001).
  5. If possible, decouple Transport Requests from the existing Change Document and Change Request (without releasing the Transport Requests).
  6. Create a new Change Request and Change Document with the new phase cycle.
  7. Reassign Transport Requests to the new Change Document.
  8. Test the connection by creating, releasing, and decoupling a dummy Transport Request (TR).
  9. Delete the old system entries (<D-SID>, <Q-SID>, <P-SID>) and associated old RFC connections in the PUM system.

Option B:

  1. Allocate the logical component group (<LCG_NAME>) to the new system entries (<D-SID>00001, <Q-SID>00001, and <P-SID>00001).
  2. De-allocate the logical component group (<LCG_NAME>) from the old system entries (<D-SID>, <Q-SID>, and <P-SID>).
  3. Rename the old systems (<D-SID>, <Q-SID>, <P-SID>) to <D-SID>00002, <Q-SID>00002, and <P-SID>00002.
  4. Rename the new systems (<D-SID>00001, <Q-SID>00001, <P-SID>00001) to <D-SID>, <Q-SID>, <P-SID>.
  5. Reassign the updated systems (<D-SID>, <Q-SID>, <P-SID>) in the phase cycle "<PHASE_CYCLE_NAME)" in the scope step and to the Build step.
  6. Test the connection by creating, releasing, and decoupling a dummy Transport Request (TR). If successful:
  7. Delete the old system entries (<D-SID>00002, <Q-SID>00002, and <P-SID>00002).

Scenario 2: Systems Already Migrated but New System Entries Not Updated in LMDB Due to Failed RFC Connection

  1. Deactivate SLD & LMDB Sync and rename the old system entries in SLD & LMDB from (<D-SID>, <Q-SID>, and <P-SID>) to <D-SID>00002, <Q-SID>00002, and <P-SID>00002.
  2. Update RFC connections manually through Tcode SM59.
  3. Re-activate SLD & LMDB Sync to update and synchronize system entries in SLD & LMDB.
  4. Allocate the existing logical component group (<LCG_NAME>) to the new system entries (<D-SID>, <Q-SID>, and <P-SID>).
  5. De-allocate the logical component group (<LCG_NAME>) from the old system entries (<D-SID>00002, <Q-SID>00002, and <P-SID>00002).
  6. Create a new phase cycle with the new systems (<D-SID>, <Q-SID>, <P-SID>).
  7. If possible, decouple Transport Requests from the existing Change Document and Change Request (without releasing the Transport Requests).
  8. Create a new Change Request and Change Document with the new phase cycle.
  9. Reassign Transport Requests to the new Change Document.
  10. Test the connection by creating, releasing, and decoupling a dummy Transport Request (TR).
  11. Delete the old system entries (<D-SID>00002, <Q-SID>00002, and <P-SID>00002).

Scenario 3: Systems Ready for Migration to SAP HANA Private Cloud/SAP2SKY with No Open Change Requests/Change Documents

  1. Request the relevant team to close any open change requests/change documents before migration begins.
  2. Deactivate SLD & LMDB Sync and delete old system entries in SLD & LMDB (<D-SID>, <Q-SID>, <P-SID>).
  3. Re-activate SLD & LMDB Sync to update and synchronize system entries in SLD & LMDB.
  4. Create new RFC connections for the new system entries (<D-SID>, <Q-SID>, <P-SID>) through Managed System Configuration. The following credentials are required from both systems:
    • <D-SID> (Development)
      • SM_<D-SID>CLNT<Client no>_READ
      • SM_<D-SID>CLNT<Client no>_TMW
      • SM_<D-SID>CLNT<Client no>_TRUSTED
      • SM_<D-SID>CLNT<Client no>_LOGIN
    • <Q-SID> (Quality)
      • SM_<Q-SID>CLNT<Client no>_READ
      • SM_<Q-SID>CLNT<Client no>_TMW
      • SM_<Q-SID>CLNT<Client no>_TRUSTED
      • SM_<Q-SID>CLNT<Client no>_LOGIN
    • <P-SID> (Production)
      • SM_<P-SID>CLNT<Client no>_READ
      • SM_<P-SID>CLNT<Client no>_TMW
      • SM_<P-SID>CLNT<Client no>_TRUSTED
      • SM_<P-SID>CLNT<Client no>_LOGIN
  5. Allocate the existing logical component group (<LCG_NAME>) to the new system entries (<D-SID>, <Q-SID>, and <P-SID>).
  6. Create a new phase cycle with the new systems (<D-SID>, <Q-SID>, <P-SID>).
  7. Create new Change Requests and Change Documents with the new phase cycle.
  8. Test the connection by creating, releasing, and decoupling a dummy Transport Request (TR).

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

Regards

Srinivas V.

entry.title: Harmonized Insights: Introducing Unified Customer Info Sheets for BTP Metrics
entry.content:
At SAP, we are committed to enhancing your user experience and providing seamless access to information that matters to your business. We are pleased to announce a step towards improving usage transparency. We have introduced Platform as a Service (PaaS) subscription metrics to our harmonized Customer Information Sheets. With this, all important metric information is now available in one place through the SAP Support Portal as well as directly in SAP for Me.

What this means for you

  1. Improved Access: All relevant metric information, including Cloud Platform Subscription Metrics, can be accessed directly through SAP for Me.
  2. Improved Transparency: Providing Customer Information Sheets for all of SAP’s public subscription metrics in a unified, streamlined format in one place reduces complexity and allows for a clearer and more comprehensive understanding of the metering logic and results.
  3. Improved Clarity and Confidence: The included instructions for independent verification of the measurement results will help boost confidence in our metering and reporting.

How to access your metric information

Getting started is easy:

  • SAP Support Portal: A list of all PaaS and SaaS Customer Info Sheets can be found on our SAP Cloud Subscription Usage page on the SAP Support Portal. The introductory text has been adjusted to include a link to search for historical documents (product-specific Supplements and Service Description Guides) in the archive, as well as a reference to the SAP Discovery Center, where descriptions for non-subscription offerings can be found.
  • SAP for Me:
    1. Log in to SAP for Me: Visit the SAP for Me portal and log in with your credentials.
    2. Navigate to Consumption: After logging in, navigate to the Consumption tab in the Finance & Legal section, where you will find all relevant metric information in the subscription context, including BTP PaaS metrics (often referred to as CP ###).
    3. Explore and analyze: Dive into the detailed Customer Information Sheets by clicking on the metric name or the link next to it to understand the metering, review the usage, and make informed decisions about your SAP subscriptions.

Elevate Your Experience with SAP for Me

SAP for Me is designed to be your digital companion for managing and optimizing your SAP solutions. With the harmonized Customer Information Sheets, we are taking another step towards delivering a unified, intuitive, and value-adding experience. We invite you to explore these enhancements and discover how SAP for Me can simplify and elevate your subscription management.

Further Information

Please use the ‘Feedback’ slider on the right edge of the SAP for Me page of the application to share your feedback with us.
Stay updated by subscribing to this blog and visiting the License Utilization Information Support portal.

SAP for Me is made for you!

entry.title: Re: impex and default value for usergroup
entry.content:
You should use the default modifier next to the uid attribute inside the parentheses. Therefore, your ImpEx header should be as follows

UPDATE Employee; uid[unique = true]; name; groups(uid[default = myUserGroup])[mode=append]; password[default = $defaultPassword]; sessionLanguage(isocode)[default=$defaultLanguage]; sessionCurrency(isocode)[default = $defaultCurrency]

entry.title: Re: how to change a fact source model from an analytic model
entry.content:
It's not possible using the Data Builder.

It took MANY YEARS for SAC to be able to change the origin of a Story, and it was something widely demanded. So if they follow the same criteria you can wait even a couple of years.

In DSP the only option is to create an EXACT copy of the current view, with the same fields, types, associations, etc.
Then you download the JSON/CSN from the AM and replace the old view name with the new one.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: SFTP adapter - how to handle errors within processing a file
entry.content:
Hi Hira,

JMS queue is perfectly fine. In case of error we send the message to JMS queue and retry from there. It should be sufficient in most cases. Only if we hit the limits of JMS queue or want an additional control over the handling, we apply custom design.
Queue capacity is 300 MB as per the documentation.
https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/jms-resource-limits-and-optimizing-their-usage

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM vs CHARM in managing one landscape with...
entry.content:
Hello Maurizio, 

Sorry for not answering in the blog.

As you can read in SAP Road Map Explorer https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=73554900100800002513&range=CURRENT-LAST#Q2%202025

CSOL is planned for Q4 2025, and after CSOL Retrofit will arrive also planned for Q4 2025:
"Cross-system object lock foundation for retrofit
Overview
  • Object locks across systems to detect whether development objects have been touched in different system landscapes
  • The locking mechanism should build the foundation for an automatic retrofit option

Benefits

Enable consistent deployment orchestration across supported transport tools. "
 
"Support for the automatic retrofit

Overview

  • Support the automatic retrofit process based on a cross-system object lock mechanism
  • Define the source and retrofit system to use SAP Cloud ALM for automating the retrofit process
  • Allow only transport objects without any conflicts to be retrofitted automatically
  • Do not provide support for a semi-automatic retrofit

Benefits

Accelerate implementation projects and reduce manual integration and configuration efforts by leveraging the suite quality “coordinated lifecycle management” for harmonized software provisioning, setup, and operations, as well as monitoring solutions, for your intelligent, sustainable enterprise"
 
Best regards,
Dolores

entry.title: how to create in SAP C4C a report that measures the days an opportunity has been in a sales phase
entry.content:

I need to create a report that measures in days how much the opportunities have been in a specific sales phase.
If you go to the different opportunities you can see the history (the dates of when they entered the phase and the end date with the relative duration).
I would like to point out that I have already configured the opportunity history in the scoping.
I tried to use the Opportunity History as a data source but it does not retrieve that data.
The problem is that when I try to create the report no data source retrieves that data. It is not possible to create Key Figures because the data available based on the different data sources do not have the necessary data (e.g. end date of phase - start date of phase).
Furthermore, I have verified that using the opportunity hader as a data source it is possible to analyze the days in which the opportunities are in a sales phase, but when they exceed it they are no longer counted.
Any suggestions?

entry.title: Re: Am not able to see coupons in promotions conditions &...
entry.content:
The condition for the coupon (ID: y_qualifying_coupons) is defined in the essentialdata-definitions.impex file, located under

<HYBRIS_BIN_DIR>/modules/coupon/couponservices/resources/couponservices/import.

This file is imported by the CouponServicesSystemSetup class during the essential data import.

Therefore, executing ant updatesystem or updating the system via HAC (under Platform > Update) while selecting the Create essential data option should create the necessary condition for the coupon.

If it is not created after the essential data update, check the logs during the system update to see if there are any error messages.

You can also try importing the ImpEx manually via HAC to see any errors immediately.

entry.title: Re: Printing a single page from multiple pages using Crystal Reports Viewer
entry.content:
Hello DonWilliams, Our environment is a desktop app.

Creating the layout of the invoice.

Visual Studio reference settings.

This is the SAP standard frmPreview.

<Global.Microsoft.VisualBasic.CompilerServices.DesignerGenerated()> _
Partial Class frmPreview
Inherits System.Windows.Forms.Form
'フォームがコンポーネントの一覧をクリーンアップするために dispose をオーバーライドします。
<System.Diagnostics.DebuggerNonUserCode()> _
Protected Overrides Sub Dispose(ByVal disposing As Boolean)
Try
If disposing AndAlso components IsNot Nothing Then
components.Dispose()
End If
Finally
MyBase.Dispose(disposing)
End Try
End Sub
'Windows フォーム デザイナーで必要です。
Private components As System.ComponentModel.IContainer
'メモ: 以下のプロシージャは Windows フォーム デザイナーで必要です。
'Windows フォーム デザイナーを使用して変更できます。
'コード エディターを使って変更しないでください。
<System.Diagnostics.DebuggerStepThrough()> _
Private Sub InitializeComponent()
Me.CrystalReportViewer1 = New CrystalDecisions.Windows.Forms.CrystalReportViewer()
Me.SuspendLayout()
'
'CrystalReportViewer1
'
Me.CrystalReportViewer1.ActiveViewIndex = -1
Me.CrystalReportViewer1.BorderStyle = System.Windows.Forms.BorderStyle.FixedSingle
Me.CrystalReportViewer1.Cursor = System.Windows.Forms.Cursors.Default
Me.CrystalReportViewer1.Dock = System.Windows.Forms.DockStyle.Fill
Me.CrystalReportViewer1.Location = New System.Drawing.Point(0, 0)
Me.CrystalReportViewer1.Name = "CrystalReportViewer1"
Me.CrystalReportViewer1.Size = New System.Drawing.Size(684, 702)
Me.CrystalReportViewer1.TabIndex = 0
Me.CrystalReportViewer1.ToolPanelView = CrystalDecisions.Windows.Forms.ToolPanelViewType.None
'
'frmPreview
'
Me.AutoScaleDimensions = New System.Drawing.SizeF(6.0!, 12.0!)
Me.AutoScaleMode = System.Windows.Forms.AutoScaleMode.Font
Me.ClientSize = New System.Drawing.Size(684, 702)
Me.Controls.Add(Me.CrystalReportViewer1)
Me.Name = "frmPreview"
Me.ResumeLayout(False)
End Sub
Friend WithEvents CrystalReportViewer1 As CrystalDecisions.Windows.Forms.CrystalReportViewer
End Class

 

This is the report preview output instruction screen.

Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On
Imports Microsoft.VisualBasic.PowerPacks.Printing.Compatibility.VB6
Imports System.Management
Imports CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine
Imports CrystalDecisions.Shared
Inherits System.Windows.Forms.Form
Public giFormName As String
Private Sub CmdBACK_Click(ByVal eventSender As System.Object, ByVal eventArgs As System.EventArgs) Handles cmdBACK.Click
Me.Dispose()
RPT0000.Show()
RPT0000.Activate()
End Sub
Private Sub CmdOUT_Click(ByVal eventSender As System.Object, ByVal eventArgs As System.EventArgs) Handles cmdOUT.Click
Dim bolReturn As Boolean
Dim intRtn As Short
Dim intMsgRtn As Short
Dim ms As New ManagementObjectSearcher("Select * from Win32_Printer")
Dim strRptPath As String
Dim strRptName As String
Dim strBegin As String
Dim strEnd As String
Dim cystlRpt As New ReportDocument()
On Error GoTo Err_section
intRtn = 0
intMsgRtn = 0
strRptPath = Mid$(My.Application.Info.DirectoryPath, 1, InStrRev(My.Application.Info.DirectoryPath, "\")) & "Reports\"
strRptName = "RPT0000.RPT"
For Each objPrinter As ManagementObject In ms.Get()
If DirectCast(objPrinter("Default"), Boolean) = True Then
Debug.WriteLine(objPrinter("Name").ToString())
cystlRpt.PrintOptions.PrinterName = objPrinter("Name").ToString()
Exit For
End If
Next objPrinter
cystlRpt.Load(strRptPath & strRptName)
Dim i As Integer = 0
For Each Table As CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine.Table In cystlRpt.Database.Tables
Dim logOnInfo = Table.LogOnInfo
logOnInfo.ConnectionInfo.DatabaseName = gDBName
logOnInfo.ConnectionInfo.ServerName = gDBName
logOnInfo.ConnectionInfo.UserID = gDBLogin
logOnInfo.ConnectionInfo.Password = gDBPassword
cystlRpt.Database.Tables.Item(i).ApplyLogOnInfo(logOnInfo)
cystlRpt.Database.Tables.Item(i).Location = gDBName & ".dbo." & Table.Name
i = i + 1
Next
frmPreview.CrystalReportViewer1.ReportSource = cystlRpt
frmPreview.CrystalReportViewer1.RefreshReport()
frmPreview.Show()
End If
Exit Sub
Err_section:
End Class

entry.title: Issue with SAP ENABLE NOW Simulation Recording on SAP RISE
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m encountering an issue when recording simulations with SAP ENABLE NOW on an SAP RISE system.

Our customer has two systems:

  • Quality system migrated to SAP RISE
  • Production system still on-premise ECC6 (migration to RISE in progress)
  • Connection via SAP LOGON on both systems

Observed Issues:

  1. Recording a simulation on the Production system (on-premise ECC6) – OK ✅
    • No issues, normal behavior
  2. Recording the same transactions from the same PC on the Quality system (RISE) – KO ❌
    • The highlighting frame around input fields is incorrectly positioned (too large, covering more than the input field).
    • Auto-generated text is incorrect: for example, instead of "The field is filled out" (Production), we get "Field is clearing" (Quality).
    • Incorrect input values displayed during playback: special characters appear instead (e.g., é-#).
    • First letter entry triggers an automatic recording (Producer bar turns red), meaning input is split into two steps: first letter → then the rest of the input.

Configuration Adjustments Already Made:

  • Transaction RZ11 settings configured.
  • SAP Logon settings adjusted according to SAP recommendations (Blue Crystal Theme, Notifications disabled, Narrow cursor, Accessibility & Scripting settings, etc.).
  • Screen scaling set to 100%.
  • Web browser zoom set to 100%.

The issue has been reproduced on multiple PCs and across different transactions (FI FB60, PP CO01, etc.).

Has anyone experienced a similar issue when using SAP ENABLE NOW on SAP RISE? Any insights or potential fixes would be greatly appreciated!

 

Regards

Mickael

entry.title: Status based form (performance improvement plan) creation - Please vote
entry.content:
For Performance documents, especially performance improvement plan, the status of the form and end dates are vital. Currently, launching of new form takes into consideration the end date. 

However, the problem is if the performance document is completed before the end date the user cannot launch a new form because the setting prevents it. 
Our request is to have the ability to also add the status into the checks, not only the end date, when allowing / denying the ability to launch a new form.
This will allow the users to create a new form even if the dates have not passed in a form that is already completed. 

There is a customer influence for this, more details can be read here - Customer Influence . If you think this will be helpful for you, please vote for this customer influence idea.

entry.title: SAP DM Automatic SSO
entry.content:
Scenario:

Automatic access to the SAP DM (Digital Manufacturing) Cloud solution deployed on SAP BTP Subaccount Cloud Foundry for workstations (Airport-style screens and Totems) without a keyboard. The additional constraint is that these workstations reboot once a day. Which is the best practice to perform automatic SSO to SAP DM?

 

Solution idea:

To automate the process, let's suppose we create an unattend desktop agent with SAP Build Process Automation that opens the browser, navigates to the application link and automatically inserts username and password. Authentication is delegated to the BTP Cloud Identity Service.

 

Questions:

In the scenario described, is the solution idea correct or are there alternative solutions that are more compliant with SAP best practices? What is the complete list of services needed to implement the solution? What costs would we have? Is an initial human intervention necessary to run the agent when the workstation reboots? Can session timeout cause problems?

entry.title: Re: Read custom fields with SCIM API V2
entry.content:
@MichaelBuonoGKN with sf.api.version = 1 it was working (our scenario is different from yours, I guess).

we added a custom field in SuccessFactors with technical name "IASsync", we added it to sf.user.attributes (userId,username,status,email,lastName,firstName,lastModifiedDateTime,personKeyNav,loginMethod,CustLoginMethod,IASsync) and then read it in the Source transformation with a basic rule:

{
"sourcePath": "$.IASsync",
"optional": true,
"targetPath": "$.IASsync"
},

then in Target transformation we filtered it out with a condition: && ($.IASsync != '2046299')

As a result, SF users with that particular value in that field were not synced to IAS.

Now with sf.api.version = 2 custom fields cannot be read anymore...SCIM API only reads the 'standard' custom fields custom01 - custom15 but our client already used all of them...

We're trying to work with group filters (like: do not sync users assigned to a certain group), but we're not lucky so far...

any thoughts on how to use the condition and which would be the right syntax?

we're trying with ($.groups[?(@.displayName == 'GG-IASSYNC')] EMPTY true) but it does not seem to work

entry.title: Re: Issue with FMS on Issue for Production Quantity
entry.content:
Hi Joseph,

if you would have tested your query as described, you would had found out that it is not working, and perhabs fixed it on your own.

The right query is 

SELECT CASE
WHEN CAST($[$13.1880000004.0] AS INT) = 4
THEN CAST(REPLACE($[$13.87.0],',','.') AS Numeric(18,4))
ELSE 0.0001
END

At the end it is perhabs not possible that it will run automatically (like copy to function in documents) because the form is already filled by SAP. Perhabs you find another field that can trigger it.

Otherwise the User has to click on the magnifying glass manually

regards Lothar

entry.title: Re: COMMIT/ROLLBACK in Custom Action in SAP RAP
entry.content:
Hi, 

Why you need COMMIT/ROLLBACK? Ideally, you should modify the entity and the SAVE-SEQUENCE will start which will save the changes post validation. Your validation should control whether your change is eligible for commit or rollback.

Thanks,

Mainak

entry.title: How to Run Black Duck Scans in SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery Jobs
entry.content:
In today's software development landscape, integrating security scans into your continuous integration and delivery (CI/CD) pipelines is a best practice. An automated process quickly identifies vulnerabilities and ensures that your software is secure. One essential tool for achieving this level of security is Black Duck. This tool performs comprehensive scans of your codebase and its open-source dependencies, helping to identify potential security vulnerabilities, license compliance issues, and code quality problems.

In this blog, I'll walk you through the process of integrating Black Duck scans into your SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery jobs. By creating a custom command for Black Duck scans, you can actively detect and address risks before they compromise your software, ensuring a more secure and reliable product. Let's dive into the steps required to achieve this integration and enhance your CI/CD pipeline with robust security measures.

Prerequisites

  1. SAP BTP Access:
    Ensure that you have access to SAP BTP and verify that you have the necessary entitlements for the SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery service. See Initial Setup.
  2. Black Duck Access:
    Make sure you have access to a Black Duck server and have your API token available.
  3. Git Repository:
    You need a Git repository connected to SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery. See Add a Repository.

Procedure

1. Prepare Your Script

We'll use a bash script to run a Black Duck scan. This script installs the necessary Java Runtime Environment (JRE), downloads the Black Duck Detect tool, and then executes the scan.

Here’s the script we'll use:

#!/bin/bash
set -eu

# Runs Black Duck scan in current directory after downloading Java 
# and Synopsys Detect script.

JRE_VERSION="21.0.5" 
JRE_TAR="sapmachine-jre-${JRE_VERSION}_linux-x64_bin.tar.gz"
JRE_URL="https://github.com/SAP/SapMachine/releases/download/sapmachine-$JRE_VERSION/$JRE_TAR"

DETECT_URL="https://detect.synopsys.com/detect10.sh" 

install_jre() {
  echo "Downloading and installing sapmachine from $JRE_URL"
  
  wget --quiet $JRE_URL -O - | tar -xzf -

  export JAVA_HOME=$(pwd)/sapmachine-jre-${JRE_VERSION}
  $JAVA_HOME/bin/java -version

  echo "Java installation complete."
}

download_detect_tool() {
  echo "Downloading Black Duck Detect tool from $DETECT_URL"

  wget --quiet $DETECT_URL -O detect.sh
  chmod +x detect.sh
  
  echo "Black Duck Detect tool download complete."
}

run_blackduck_scan() {
  echo "Running Black Duck scan..."

  ./detect.sh --blackduck.url="$BD_SERVER" --blackduck.api.token="$BD_API_TOKEN" --detect.project.name="$BD_PROJECT_NAME" --detect.project.version.name="$BD_PROJECT_VERSION"
  echo "Black Duck scan completed."
}

# Main script execution
install_jre
download_detect_tool
run_blackduck_scan
2. Upload the Script to Your Repository

Add your script to the repository containing your project source code.

3. Add Additional Commands to Your Job

  1. In SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery, either create a new job or navigate to the job to which you want to add additional commands and choose Edit.

  2. In the Compliance stage section of your job, choose + next to Additional Commands. This allows you to introduce a custom script.

  3. Choose either Run First in Stage or Run Last in Stage to execute the scan in relation to other tasks.

  4. In the Command text field. enter the following command to execute your script:

    bash path/to/your/blackduck_script.sh

  5. Replace path/to/your/blackduck_script.sh with the actual path to your script in the repository.

  6. Confirm your changes.

4. Add Additional Variables to Your Job

The script uses a couple of variables provided in the environment, so you don't need to adjust the script for your jobs. Instead, we will set these variables using the Additional Variables feature.

  1. In the Compliance stage section of your job, choose + next to Additional Variables. This opens the Add Variables pop-up.

  2. Add the following variables one after the other:

    • BD_SERVER: Your Black Duck server URL.
    • BD_PROJECT_NAME: The name of your project in Black Duck.
    • BD_PROJECT_VERSION: The version of the project you are scanning.

These variables will be used as parameters for the detect.sh script.

5. Add Additional Credentials to Your Job

In the Compliance stage section of your job, choose + next to Additional Credentials and add the following credential to access your Black Duck server:

BD_API_TOKEN: Your Black Duck API token.

6. Test Your Configuration

Once the setup is complete, trigger your pipeline either manually or by committing new code to your repository. Monitor the pipeline to ensure the Black Duck scan executes properly and review the logs for any errors or output from the scan.

Conclusion

With these steps, you've successfully integrated Black Duck scans into your SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery pipeline. You've empowered your development process with a robust security mechanism that proactively identifies vulnerabilities and maintains code quality. By automating these scans, you can focus on delivering secure, reliable software faster. Stay ahead of potential threats and ensure your software meets the highest security standards with this seamless integration. Happy secure coding!

entry.title: High-Impact Use Cases of SAP Joule AI Agents
entry.content:
​In the dynamic landscape of enterprise technology, SAP's Joule AI agents are poised to revolutionize business operations, particularly within the S/4HANA ecosystem. These intelligent agents are not just tools but collaborative partners designed to automate complex workflows across various business functions, thereby enhancing efficiency and decision-making.​

The integration of Joule AI agents into SAP's S/4HANA platform signifies a monumental shift towards intelligent automation. By addressing critical areas such as procurement inefficiencies, contract compliance, and payment processing, these agents empower organizations to operate with unprecedented agility and precision.

Look below for use cases that fit your organization! Standard SAP Joule Agents will be coming later in 2025.

For each case, we provide:
- A short description of the challenge.
- A proposition of what the agent would do.
- Estimated benefits (on the right of the screen!).
- Exactly which transactions or actions Joule would take (on the right of the screen!).

We will update it with references and learnings soon.
Thanks to @Hachem Bouabid and @Jose Ignacio Cabal for collaborating on this list!

Source to Pay:

Use CaseDescriptionPropositionEstimated benefitsWhat Joule does
1.
Maverick buying		Unapproved purchases made outside of established procurement processes, leading to higher costs and inefficienciesAn SAP Joule agent can continuously monitor purchase orders and spending patterns to detect out-of-policy transactions in real time. By flagging these and suggesting approved vendors, it helps enforce compliance and reduce off-contract spending.People Involved: ~10–30 (procurement, finance, line-of-business managers)
Potential Value/ROI: 1–3% of total procurement spend recovered or prevented; for $100M spend, that can be $1M–$3M saved annually
FTE Saved: ~2–4 through reduced manual oversight, fewer policy violations to chase		The agent monitors purchase orders (e.g., via ME23N) and compares them to approved vendor lists and contract terms. It automatically flags off-contract purchases and triggers an email alert to the responsible manager, suggesting a corrective purchase requisition (ME51N) aligned with approved sources.
2.
Contract usage		Ensuring purchases align with negotiated contracts to maximize savings and complianceThe agent can cross-reference negotiated contracts with actual purchasing data to ensure that all spend is aligned with the best possible terms. Automated alerts and analytics help drive compliance, thereby maximizing savings and reducing risk.People Involved: ~5–15 (procurement, contract management, finance)
Potential Value/ROI: 1–2% improvement in cost savings by ensuring compliance; up to $1M–$2M on a $100M contract portfolio
FTE Saved: ~1–3 from reduced contract auditing and manual tracking		The agent scans active contracts in SAP (e.g., ME33K) and matches them against new POs. It sends a system notification to buyers when a purchase is made outside the negotiated terms and proposes corrective actions like contract call-offs or PO amendments.
3.
Late payments		Delays in invoice payments, potentially leading to penalties, external dunning efforts and strained supplier relationships By integrating with accounts payable data, the Joule agent can predict upcoming due dates, highlight at-risk invoices, and recommend optimal payment scheduling. This ensures timely payments, reduces penalty fees, and maintains strong supplier relationships.People Involved: ~5–15 (accounts payable, treasury, finance)
Potential Value/ROI: Reduced penalties and strained supplier relationships; can save $100K–$500K in penalty fees and negotiation costs
FTE Saved: ~1–2 by automating reminders and prioritizing payment scheduling		The agent continuously checks open invoices in the Accounts Payable module (e.g., FBL1N) and identifies invoices nearing due dates. It triggers reminders to the AP team or auto-generates payment proposals (F110) to ensure timely settlement and avoid penalties.
4.
Lost cash discount		Missing out on early payment discountsThe agent can identify potential early-payment discounts by comparing invoice terms with real-time cash-flow forecasts. This proactive approach helps organizations capitalize on discount opportunities and optimize working capital.People Involved: ~5–10 (accounts payable, treasury)
Potential Value/ROI: Recover 0.5–1% of spend via early payment discounts; for $100M, up to $1M annually
FTE Saved: ~1–2 by automating discount tracking and invoice scheduling		The agent reviews invoice payment terms (e.g., via MIRO data) and forecasts cash flow to determine if early payment discounts are feasible. It then schedules or suggests payment runs (F110) before discount deadlines and sends a notification to AP if additional approvals are needed.
5.
Duplicate invoices		Identifying and preventing duplicate payments to avoid financial leakage.SAP Joule can use pattern recognition and invoice matching to detect duplicates, preventing financial leakage. This real-time scanning reduces manual checking effort and ensures higher data accuracy.People Involved: ~5–10 (accounts payable, finance)
Potential Value/ROI: Avoid 0.1–0.5% in erroneous payments; up to $100K–$500K on $100M spend
FTE Saved: ~1–2 from reduced manual invoice checks and dispute resolution		By analyzing invoice data across multiple fields (e.g., in MIR4 or FB03), the agent detects duplicate entries. It blocks duplicate documents automatically and emails a summary report to the finance team for review and resolution.
6.
Automation of purchase order creation		Streamlining PO generation to improve efficiency and reduce manual effortThe agent can analyze past purchasing data, vendor catalogs, and usage patterns to auto-generate purchase orders aligned with demand forecasts. This reduces manual entry errors, speeds up the procurement cycle, and frees up procurement staff for more strategic tasks.People Involved: ~5–15 (procurement, finance)
Potential Value/ROI: Faster cycle times, fewer errors; often 10–30% efficiency gain in PO processing
FTE Saved: ~2–4 by reducing manual data entry and repetitive tasks		The agent leverages historical procurement data and vendor info (e.g., via ME2N) to auto-generate new POs (ME21N). It populates relevant fields, attaches any necessary documents, and notifies buyers for a quick review and approval.
7.
Changes in purchase orders		Reducing non-value-adding modifications to maintain process efficiency.The agent can track and analyze frequent change requests, identifying root causes and suggesting standardized processes to minimize disruptions. This not only reduces manual rework but also ensures more consistent lead times and supplier relationships.People Involved: ~5–10 (procurement, requestors, suppliers)
Potential Value/ROI: 10–20% fewer PO modifications, improving supplier relationships and cycle time
FTE Saved: ~1–2 by minimizing administrative rework and approvals		The agent tracks frequent change requests on POs (e.g., ME22N) and analyzes the root causes. It sends an automated alert or report to procurement leaders, suggesting process improvements to minimize recurring manual changes.
8.
Payment terms discrepancy		Mismatches between agreed payment terms and actual payment execution (PO vs. Invoice)By comparing contract terms, purchase orders, and invoices, the agent flags mismatches in payment terms. This timely insight ensures consistent cash flow planning and prevents costly errors.People Involved: ~5–10 (accounts payable, finance, procurement)
Potential Value/ROI: 0.5–1% improvement in cash-flow management; reduces interest or penalty risks
FTE Saved: ~1–2 through automated matching of PO, invoice, and contract terms		The agent compares PO payment terms (ME23N) to invoice details (MIRO) and contract data (ME33K). It flags any mismatches, sends an email to the responsible buyer or AP clerk, and proposes correcting entries in the PO or invoice as needed.
9.
Avoid early payments		Preventing premature payments that negatively impact cash flow.The Joule agent can track invoice due dates and align them with cash management strategies, ensuring payments are not processed prematurely. This helps maintain optimal working capital and avoids unplanned outflows of cash.People Involved: ~5–10 (treasury, accounts payable)
Potential Value/ROI: 0.5–1% improvement in working capital utilization; potentially $500K–$1M freed up
FTE Saved: ~1–2 by automating payment scheduling aligned with optimal cash flow		The agent monitors due dates in the Accounts Payable ledger (FBL1N) and cross-checks them with company cash management schedules. It holds payment proposals (F110) until the optimal date and sends a notification to the finance team if any manual intervention is required.
10.
Early deliveries		Receiving goods before the expected date, which may negatively impact cash flow and disrupt inventory planningBy monitoring production schedules and supplier lead times, the agent can anticipate and flag early shipments that might disrupt inventory planning. This enables timely interventions, reducing excess holding costs and aligning deliveries with demand.People Involved: ~5–15 (supply chain, logistics, procurement)
Potential Value/ROI: 5–10% reduction in excess inventory holding costs; can equate to $100K–$500K in a mid-size operation
FTE Saved: ~1–2 by reducing the need for ad-hoc inventory re-planning		The agent reviews planned delivery dates in the PO (ME23N) and compares them to goods receipts (MIGO). It flags items that arrive too early and sends a warning email to procurement or warehouse teams, suggesting schedule adjustments or renegotiation with the supplier.

 

Lead to Cash:

Use CaseDescriptionPropositionEstimated benefitsWhat Joule does
11.
Shipped not billed items		Goods delivered but not yet invoicedThe Joule agent can cross-check shipping and billing data, detecting gaps where items have been delivered but not invoiced. Automated notifications ensure timely billing, improving cash flow and revenue recognition.People Involved: ~5–10 (sales ops, billing, finance)
Potential Value/ROI: Improves revenue recognition speed; could unlock $100K–$1M in unbilled revenue, depending on volumes
FTE Saved: ~1–2 through automated billing triggers and reconciliation		The agent matches outbound deliveries (VL03N) to billing documents (VF03) and detects any gaps. It triggers an automated workflow to create pending billing documents (VF01) and sends a reminder email to the billing clerk to finalize invoices.
12.
Rejected sales document items 		Orders rejected by customers or internal processes, impacting revenue flowThe agent analyzes reasons for rejections, identifies patterns (e.g., pricing errors or missing data), and recommends corrective actions to prevent future issues. This reduces revenue leakage and shortens the order-to-cash cycle.People Involved: ~5–10 (sales ops, customer service, finance)
Potential Value/ROI: Reduces revenue leakage by catching and resolving issues quickly; could recover 1–2% of potential sales
FTE Saved: ~1–2 by automating error detection and resolution workflows		By analyzing sales order rejections in VA02, the agent identifies common error patterns like pricing or material availability. It sends a consolidated report to sales management, suggesting template corrections or master data updates to reduce future rejections.
13.
Unearned cash discount		Customers applying early payment discounts without meeting eligibility criteriaBy comparing actual payment timings with contractual discount eligibility, the agent can detect unauthorized discount deductions. This ensures accurate billing, protects margins, and fosters transparent customer relationships.People Involved: ~5–10 (accounts receivable, finance, customer service)
Potential Value/ROI: 0.5–1% of invoice value reclaimed if customers incorrectly apply discounts
FTE Saved: ~1–2 by automating discount eligibility checks and dispute handling		The agent compares actual payment dates (FBL5N) with the discount eligibility rules (customer master data) and identifies unauthorized deductions. It automatically generates debit memos (VF01) or sends a notification to the AR team for manual review and correction.
14.
Automation of sales document creation		Reducing manual effort by auto-generating sales orders and invoicesUsing historical order data, product catalogs, and customer preferences, the agent can generate sales documents automatically. This cuts down manual workload, shortens processing time, and reduces errors.People Involved: ~5–15 (sales ops, customer service, finance)
Potential Value/ROI: 10–20% faster order processing; fewer manual errors leading to higher customer satisfaction
FTE Saved: ~2–3 by eliminating repetitive data entry tasks		Using historical sales data and customer preferences, the agent generates new sales orders (VA01) with pre-populated fields. It emails the sales team for confirmation and pushes the order to the standard workflow if no exceptions are detected.
15.
Automation of Sales Billing Documents Creation		Streamlining billing processes to reduce manual effort and accelerate revenue recognitionThe Joule agent can seamlessly convert confirmed sales orders into billing documents with minimal human intervention. This accelerates the revenue cycle and ensures consistent, accurate invoicing.People Involved: ~5–10 (sales ops, billing, finance)
Potential Value/ROI: Accelerated billing and revenue recognition; 10–20% reduction in manual billing workload
FTE Saved: ~2–4 due to fully automated creation of invoices/billing documents		Once a sales order is marked as delivered (VL01N/VL02N), the agent triggers billing document creation (VF01) without manual intervention. It updates the financial records in real time and emails the invoice to the customer.
16.
Automation of customer invoice clearing		Accelerating cash application by automating invoice-to-payment matching.By matching payments to invoices based on reference numbers, amounts, and timing, the agent drastically reduces manual reconciliation effort. This frees up finance teams to focus on higher-value activities and ensures up-to-date ledger information.People Involved: ~5–10 (accounts receivable, finance)
Potential Value/ROI: 10–30% faster payment reconciliation; reduces days sales outstanding (DSO)
FTE Saved: ~2–3 by automating matching of payments to open invoices		The agent matches incoming payments (FF68/FB05) to open invoices by cross-referencing amounts and reference numbers. It clears items automatically (F-28) and sends a notification to AR staff if any discrepancies require manual handling.
17.
Late payments		Customers failing to pay on time, affecting cash flow and working capitalThe agent can track customer due dates, send proactive reminders, and analyze payment trends to identify potential defaults. By improving collection processes, it bolsters cash flow and reduces days sales outstanding (DSO).People Involved: ~5–15 (accounts receivable, collections, finance)
Potential Value/ROI: 1–2% improvement in cash flow by reducing overdue receivables; could mean $1M–$2M freed up for a $100M AR portfolio
FTE Saved: ~2–3 through automated dunning, reminders, and prioritization		The agent monitors open items in Accounts Receivable (FBL5N) and identifies overdue invoices. It automatically sends reminders to customers via email, schedules follow-up calls for the collections team, and updates the customer contact log (BP transaction or interaction record).
18.
Payment terms discrepancy		Mismatches between agreed and actual payment terms in sales transactions (Billing doc. vs Master data vs. Invoice)SAP Joule can cross-verify payment terms in the billing documents with the master data and highlight any inconsistencies. This ensures billing accuracy, fosters predictable cash flows, and strengthens customer relationships.People Involved: ~5–10 (accounts receivable, finance, sales ops)
Potential Value/ROI: 0.5–1% improvement in cash flow by ensuring correct payment terms; fewer disputes and faster collection
FTE Saved: ~1–2 by reducing manual checks and reconciliation of billing documents		By comparing billing documents (VF03) with master data (XD03) and sales orders (VA03), the agent highlights mismatched payment terms. It either proposes an adjustment in the billing document or triggers an email to the sales admin team for review.
19.
Late goods issue (Goods movement)		Delays in delivering goods to customers, impacting cash flowBy monitoring warehouse operations and order timelines, the agent can flag orders at risk of delayed dispatch. Early interventions ensure on-time deliveries, reducing backlog and maintaining healthy working capital cycles.People Involved: ~5–15 (warehouse, logistics, sales ops)
Potential Value/ROI: Minimizes shipping delays that stall invoicing; could improve cash flow by $100K–$500K depending on order volumes
FTE Saved: ~1–2 by preventing back-and-forth between logistics and sales		The agent checks planned shipment dates in outbound deliveries (VL01N/VL02N) and compares them to actual goods issue postings. It flags delays, sends an alert to the warehouse manager, and updates a tracking report in SAP for timely corrective actions.
20.
Customer invoices open and overdue		Outstanding invoices not paid within agreed terms, leading to potential bad debt or late paymentsThe agent provides real-time insights into overdue invoices, prioritizing follow-up actions based on risk levels and historical payment behavior. By automating reminders and escalation workflows, it mitigates bad-debt risk and enhances compliance.People Involved: ~5–15 (accounts receivable, collections, finance)
Potential Value/ROI: 1–3% improvement in receivables turnover; potentially $1M–$3M improvement in liquidity on $100M AR
FTE Saved: ~2–4 by automating follow-up actions, escalations, and credit risk checks		The agent reviews the AR ledger (FBL5N) and identifies overdue invoices. It sends automated dunning notices (F150) or personalized email reminders, prioritizing customers based on risk scores and past payment behavior, and escalates critical cases to management.

What would you add?

See you soon!

entry.title: Re: Schema and PCR for Absence deduction
entry.content:
Why cannot we use 1-day Unpaid leave or absence in IT2001 which will prorate the values accordingly.

You need to check the following for deduction of salary for one day

1. Absence type assignment to Unpaid valuation rule in table V_554S_G

2. Valuation Rule should be Ticked as Unpaid

3. PRCL class 10 for WTs which needs to be deducted should be 1 which means it will be processsed via PCR XPPF

entry.content: Unfortunately not, there is a restriction in place to see only 50.
